title: Paris Could Hit 41°C Monday. France Has Never Seen This.
script: Paris: 40 degrees Monday? That's not a typo. France is in its second heatwave of 2026, and meteorologists say Monday could be the hottest June day ever recorded — hitting 41°C in Paris, possibly 42°C in Nevers. 60 French departments are under orange alert. France's biggest summer music festival got cancelled in several cities. Schools are scrambling. Trains are slowing down to stop tracks from warping. The town of Montmorillon hit 40.2°C on Thursday. Saint-Florent-sur-Cher hit 40.4°C. And this is only June. France's all-time heat record is 45.9°C — set in 2019 during a week that killed hundreds. Forecasters aren't ruling out another run at it. So: which French city breaks the June record first?
